skill.name: fix-review
skill.description: Verify fix commits address audit findings without new bugs
skill.summary: Review fix commits to ensure audit findings are properly resolved without introducing regressions
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The detected URLs are documentation references in markdown, not executable network code. The weak crypto warning on line 3 is incorrect - that line contains only a description string. This skill is documentation-only with no executable code, presenting no security risk.

content.user_title: Verify Fix Commits Address Audit Findings
content.value_statement: Security fixes require careful validation to ensure they resolve issues without introducing new bugs. This skill reviews fix commits against audit findings to confirm proper remediation.
actual_capabilities: Compare fix commits against original audit findings to verify resolution
Check that fixes address root causes rather than symptoms
Identify potential side effects or new vulnerabilities introduced by fixes
Validate test coverage for fixed scenarios
Detect similar vulnerability patterns elsewhere in the codebase
limitations: Cannot execute or test code changes - analysis is static only
Requires access to both the original audit report and fix commits
May miss context-specific business logic vulnerabilities
Cannot verify runtime behavior or performance impact of fixes

use_cases.title: Security Team Lead
use_cases.description: Review developer commits that claim to fix penetration test findings before merging to main branch
use_cases.target_user: Security team leads managing remediation workflows

use_cases.title: Compliance Officer
use_cases.description: Validate that audit findings from external assessors have been properly addressed before compliance sign-off

use_cases.title: Development Team
use_cases.description: Self-review fix commits before submitting for security team approval to reduce iteration cycles

prompt_templates.title: Basic Fix Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Review commit {commit_hash} and verify it addresses audit finding {finding_id}. Check that the fix resolves the root cause and does not introduce new issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick verification of a single fix commit

prompt_templates.title: Full Remediation Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze all commits in branch {branch_name} created to address security audit report {report_id}. For each finding, verify the fix is complete, test coverage exists, and no similar vulnerabilities remain in the codebase.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive review of a remediation branch

prompt_templates.title: Regression Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the code changes in {diff_or_commit} against the original vulnerability description. Identify any potential regressions, side effects, or new attack vectors the fix might introduce.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deep dive into fix quality and side effects

prompt_templates.title: Pattern-Based Review
prompt_templates.prompt: The audit found {vulnerability_type} vulnerabilities at these locations: {locations}. Review commit {commit_hash} and identify if the same pattern exists elsewhere in the codebase that was missed.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding similar vulnerabilities across the codebase

output_examples.input: Review commit abc123 fixing SQL injection in user search endpoint
output_examples.output: Fix verified: Parameterized queries replace string concatenation. Test coverage added for injection attempts. No similar patterns found in adjacent query functions.

output_examples.input: Validate fixes for XSS findings in dashboard components
output_examples.output: 3 of 5 findings resolved. Components A and B properly escape output. Components C, D, E still vulnerable - fix incomplete. Recommend blocking merge until all instances addressed.

best_practices: Always compare fixes against the original audit finding description to ensure complete resolution
Verify that test cases specifically cover the attack scenario that was fixed
Search the codebase for similar patterns that may require the same fix
anti_patterns: Accepting fixes that only address symptoms without fixing root causes
Merging partial fixes that leave similar vulnerabilities in other locations
Skipping regression testing after security fixes are applied

faq.question: What information do I need to provide for a fix review?
faq.answer: Provide the commit hash or branch containing fixes, and reference the original audit finding ID or description. Access to the full audit report improves review accuracy.

faq.question: Can this skill verify that a fix actually works?
faq.answer: This skill performs static analysis to verify fix completeness and correctness. It cannot execute tests but can verify that test coverage was added for the fixed scenario.

faq.question: How do I know if a fix introduces new vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: The review analyzes code changes for common regression patterns, checks for proper input validation, and identifies potential side effects from the fix approach.

faq.question: What if the fix only partially addresses a finding?
faq.answer: The review will flag incomplete fixes and identify which aspects remain unaddressed. Partial fixes should not be merged until all aspects are resolved.

faq.question: Can this skill find similar vulnerabilities elsewhere in the codebase?
faq.answer: Yes, the review includes pattern matching to identify similar vulnerability patterns in other files that may have been missed in the original audit.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for compliance documentation?
faq.answer: Yes, the review produces documented evidence of fix verification that can be included in compliance artifacts and audit trails for regulatory requirements.
